commit.message:
Add FastAPI backend with ACLED/GDELT integration and severity scoring (#2)

* Add FastAPI backend with ACLED/GDELT integration and severity scoring

- ACLED API service with OAuth authentication for conflict event data
- GDELT API service for contextual news articles with tone analysis
- Composite severity score algorithm (1-10) weighing deadliness, civilian danger, event frequency, geographic spread, and trend direction
- GET /api/conflicts returns GeoJSON with severity scores
- GET /api/news returns GDELT articles with average tone
- Supabase caching layer for events and AI blurbs
- 35 tests covering models, severity algorithm, and API endpoints

- schemas/email_subscribers.sql: Supabase table schema

commit.message:
feat: implement GatherBlockQuantized operator (#673)

* feat: implement GatherBlockQuantized operator

Add support for the com.microsoft::GatherBlockQuantized contrib operator
which combines gather and block dequantization in a single operation.

Supports:
- Packed data (UINT8 containing 4-bit values) and native sub-byte types (INT4, UINT4)
- 4-bit and 8-bit quantization
- Optional zero_points (packed or unpacked)
- Configurable gather_axis and quantize_axis
- Default zero_point of 2^(bits-1) for unsigned data types

Major UI overhaul: TemplateEditor, DQ Dashboard, Dockerfile

TemplateEditor:
- File-explorer layout (Seiten/Vorlagen/Konfiguration)
- Verwendungen unter Seite ausklappen
- Drag & Drop Vorlage → Seite
- Vorlage umbenennen, löschen; Verwendung entfernen

- Rechtsklick → 'UKey mappen…' im Monaco-Kontextmenü
- Undo/Redo für Seiten-Config
- Split-View Preview mit Auto-Refresh
- Versionshistorie (🕐 Verlauf-Modal)
- Verwendungen aus Vorlagenliste herausfiltern

DataQualityDashboard (neu):
- UKEY-Suche + Fortschrittsbalken
- Drilldown-Tabs: Fehlend / Vorhanden mit Wertanzeige
- ✏ Direkt zu Mapping Config navigieren

Layout: horizontale Top-Navigation
client.ts: deleteVorlage, loadVorlageHistory, loadSkuValues, SkuValue
Dockerfile + nginx.conf für Container-Deploy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Mailgun email notifications for contributions and messages

New Mailgun client library (TheDiscDb.Mailgun) for sending email via
the Mailgun v3 API. Conditional registration: uses real service when
Mailgun:ApiKey is configured, no-op NullContributionNotificationService
otherwise.

Email notifications:
- Contribution submitted for review (admin + user confirmation)
- Contribution imported (user notification with link to item)
- Message from user to admin (admin notification)
- Message from admin to user (user notification with messages link)

Email features:
- Branded layout with TheDiscDb logo header and footer
- Markdown rendering in message emails (Markdig with DisableHtml)
- Plain-text fallback auto-generated from HTML
- Open/click tracking enabled
- Custom variables (contribution-id, notification-type) for webhooks
- List-Unsubscribe headers for inbox placement
- Reply-To headers (admin emails reply to user, user emails to admin)
- Brand color #00213F used consistently

Key fixes during development:
- Await notifications before navigation (fire-and-forget killed emails)
- Move notifications into MessageService (single notification source)
- Remove duplicate sends after merge conflict

Includes 3 new unit tests verifying exactly-one notification per message.

pdf-object: implement ASCII85Decode filter

Add `Filter::ASCII85Decode` variant, map the `"ASCII85Decode"` name in
the `From` impl, and implement `Filter::decode_ascii85` using Horner's
method with wrapping arithmetic. Wire it into `StreamObject::data()`.

Includes 7 unit tests covering full groups, the `z` shorthand, partial
final groups, whitespace skipping, the `~>` EOD marker, and invalid
character errors.

commit.message:
Fix return-immediately test: accept any state, only validate timing

Per the A2A spec, returnImmediately means the server should respond
quickly — it has no opinion on what task state is returned. The test
now passes if the response arrives within 3 seconds, regardless of
whether the state is Submitted, Working, or Completed.

Updated across all 7 client test suites: .NET, Go, Python, Java,
JavaScript, Rust, and Swift.
